definition: Approximately 180 Mtpa of LNG capacity is under construction against ~470 Mtpa existing (35%+ increase), with physically observable projects on known timelines. This wave will likely create a buyer's market by 2028 with a projected 40-60 Mtpa capacity overshoot exceeding demand growth. TTF already down from >€300/MWh peak to €30-40 range.
evidence: ~180 Mtpa under construction (Golden Pass, Plaquemines, North Field East/South)
Not directly contested by other agents
open_questions: Will South/SE Asian demand growth absorb more capacity than projected?
Could project delays narrow the overshoot?
